page: Amazon Web Services c6id.24xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz. It features 96 virtual CPUs across 48 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. The memory configuration consists of 192.0 GB of DDR4 RAM operating at 3 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of memory per physical core. Local storage is bundled, offering 5700 GB of NVMe SSD capacity. The instance operates on the Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 38 Gbps.

Benchmark data shows strong multi-core CPU performance, with stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU scores placing in the top 25%. Single-core performance is average, as reflected in the stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core results. Cryptographic capabilities are highly efficient, with the PassMark CPU encryption test ranking in the top 10%, aided by hardware-accelerated AES and VAES instruction sets. Memory bandwidth is consistently strong across both small cached blocks and large uncached blocks.

The server demonstrates a clear tradeoff in large-scale machine learning tasks. While prompt processing for small and medium language models is average to strong, text generation for large 70B models is weak, falling in the bottom 25% due to the lack of dedicated GPU hardware. However, the combination of high-capacity local NVMe storage and strong multi-threaded capabilities makes this instance suitable for database operations, software compilation, image processing, and static web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services c6id.24xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 96 vCPUs (48 physical cores) powered by dedicated 3.5 GHz Intel Xeon 8375C processors. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, running on the Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 38 Gbps. Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core performance, with PassMark CPU and multi-threaded decompression tests placing in the top 25%, and image processing and encryption benchmarks reaching the top 10%. Conversely, single-core performance remains average, and large-scale LLM text generation is weak due to the absence of dedicated GPU accelerators. This resource density and high-speed local storage make the instance highly efficient for multi-threaded workloads, including database operations, software compilation, and static web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6id.24xlarge is a compute-optimized server with 96 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and 5700 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong multi-core and cryptographic performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6id.24xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 96 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 5700 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 server featuring high-capacity local NVMe storage, strong multi-core processing, and high-speed cryptographic acceleration.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs powered by dedicated 3.5 GHz Intel Xeon 8375C processors
192.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Strong multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark results
Top-tier performance in cryptographic encryption and image processing
Weak text generation performance for large-scale language models
Suited for database operations, software compilation, and web serving
